parsed_review.summary: CP00-605 is a descriptor-only closeout pack (RP19.P08.M05.S20-RP19.P08.M06.S07, risk A, 10 units, impl:3/hermes:7) and its conservative production_ready:false / runtime_ready:false claim is fully supported. Baseline facts match across prompt, review-baseline, manifest, command-evidence, contract, closeout-pack-plan, and descriptor code (incl. ledger SHA 13c7ed65...). All 7 required public exports exist (createClientPortalCoreCp605P08PermissionFixtureSliceDescriptor/CaseSet in service.js; validateClientPortalCoreCp605Coverage, validateClientPortalCoreCp605P08PermissionFixtureSliceDescriptor, createClientPortalCoreCp605HermesEvidencePacket/ClaudeReviewPacket/CloseoutHandoff in validators.js) and surface via index.js export *. No fs/network/exec/process I/O exists in packages/client-portal/src or the validator script; functions are pure Object.freeze data construction, supporting the no-write/no-real-data attestation. The NO_WRITE_ATTESTATION spread chain CP583->...->CP605 is intact with promotes_claude_to_final_approval:false and claims_enterprise_trust_from_local_validation:false. Authority boundary holds: descriptor authority_boundary.claude_is_final_approval:false, local_validation_claims_enterprise_trust:false, human_final_approval_required_for_runtime_opening:true. Hermes evidence packet is deterministic control-plane metadata (gate H19, coverage/slice validity, no_real_data, emits_hermes_runtime_receipt:false, receipt_shape descriptor_only_..._no_write), not product runtime. Tests cover coverage/distribution, descriptor-only rows, and authority boundaries; the validator deep-equals the contract's current_pack, cp605_requirements, cp605_no_write_attestation, and p08_permission_fixture_slice_descriptor, all present and consistent in the contract; README #cp00-605 matches the documentation_entry ref. Review-evidence integrity (CP178+) is sound: readiness auth-preflight passed (exit 0, valid JSON, 1434B stdout, 0 stderr), validation-report shows schema present, no forbidden tools, prompt within size; no auth failure, 0-byte, malformed/fenced/tool-shaped output, or missing fields. No pre-existing raw-output/receipt/claude-review-result, so the review is genuinely pending and Claude remains a read-only independent reviewer, not final approver. Changed-file scope is accurate: the 7 in-scope tracked edits match active_pack_implementation_refs; unrelated dirty files (matter workbook, progress-control-room, docx) are correctly preserved and not attributed to this pack. Validation commands could not be executed (read-only, no Bash); command-evidence.json claims all passed with exit_code 0, and the code structure independently supports those claims. No defects found; human final approval remains required for any enterprise-trust/runtime-opening claim.
